content.text:
Course:
Information Systems (IS) — Semester 3, Unit UFC3, Credits: 4, Coeff: 2
Course Objectives
This course provides students with a foundational understanding of Information Systems (IS) and their role in organizations. Students will explore various techniques for information representation, methods for capturing and controlling information, and comprehensive IS development methodologies. The course also integrates additional essential topics such as IS strategy and planning, ethics and security in IS, and emerging trends in the field.
Course Content
Chapter 1: Introduction to Information Systems
Definition and Importance of IS; Components of IS: People, Process, Technology; Role and importance of IS in organizations.
Types of IS: Transaction Processing Systems (TPS), Management Information Systems (MIS), Decision Support Systems (DSS), Executive Information Systems (EIS).
Evolution of IS: Historical Development, Impact on business processes and decision-making, Trends (Cloud Computing, Mobile Computing, IoT).
Chapter 2: Information Representation Techniques
Data vs. Information vs. Knowledge; Binary and Decimal Systems; Data Encoding Techniques.
Information Modeling: Conceptual, logical, and physical data models; Flowcharts; Entity-Relationship Diagrams (ERDs); UML Basics.
Advanced Representation Techniques: Semantic Representation (Ontologies, Taxonomies); Knowledge Representation (Concept Maps, Frame-Based Systems).
Chapter 3: Information Capture and Control
Input Devices and Techniques (Scanners, Sensors, RFID, OCR); Data Entry Methods; Data Quality and Validation.
Access Control Mechanisms (Authentication, Authorization, Encryption); Audit Trails and Logging; Information Lifecycle Management.
Chapter 4: IS Development Methodologies
Traditional vs. Modern Approaches (Waterfall, Agile); Role of Prototyping.
MERISE Methodology: Introduction, Phases (Conceptual, Organizational, Logical Levels), Case Studies.
SSADM: Overview, Key Stages, Comparison with MERISE.
Object-Oriented Methodologies: OOAD, Unified Process (UP).
Agile Methodologies: Scrum and Extreme Programming (XP).
Emerging IS Methodologies: Model-Driven Architecture (MDA), Domain-Driven Design (DDD).
Chapter 5: Integration and Future Directions in Information Systems
Systems Integration Approaches (Middleware, Web Services, API Management).
Future Trends: Artificial Intelligence in IS; Quantum Computing and its Impact on IS.
Teaching Mode
Face to face mode (100%)
Assessment Mode
Exam (60%), Continuous assessment (40%)
Recommended Textbooks
Laudon, Kenneth C., & Laudon, Jane P. (2022). Management Information Systems: Managing the Digital Firm (17th ed.). Pearson.
Alter, Steven. (2023). Information Systems: Foundations of E-Business (5th ed.). Pearson.
Galliers, Robert D., & Leidner, Dorothy E. (2024). Strategic Information Management (5th ed.). Routledge.
O’Brien, James A., & Marakas, George M. (2023). Introduction to Information Systems (17th ed.). McGraw-Hill Education.
Beynon-Davies, Paul. (2022). Business Information Systems (4th ed.). Palgrave Macmillan.
Rainer, R. Kelly, Prince, Brad, & Cegielski, Casey. (2023). Introduction to Information Systems (8th ed.). Wiley.
Hirschheim, Rudy, Heinzl, Armin, & Dibbern, Jens. (2024). Information Systems Outsourcing (3rd ed.). Springer.
